revision.text:
[[de:Amun-Re]][[en:Amun-Ra]][[eo:Amon-Reo]][[fi:Amon-Ra]][[fr:Amon-Rê]][[hr:Amon Ra]][[it:Amon-Ra]][[lt:Amonas Ra]][[mg:Amôna-Ra]][[nl:Amon-Ra]][[sh:Amon-Ra]][[tl:Amun-Re]] {{This article|deals with the ancient Egyptian god Amun-Re. For the eponymous game, see {{ill|Amun-Re (board game)|en|Amun-Re (board game)|qid=Q481568|lt=Amun-Re (Game)|1=Amun-Re (board game)}}.|qq=}} {{Infobox deity |TITLE = Amun-Re |IDEOGRAM = C12-C1 |NAME-EXTENDED = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt|1=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME = M17-Y5:N35-M17-M17-C2 |NAME3-EXPLANATION = Amun-Re, Amen-Re
''Jmn-Rˁ''
''{{ill|Solar deity|en|Solar deity|qid=Q511056|lt=Ra|1=Solar deity}}, the Hidden One'' |NAME-WITH-DETERMINATIVE = |NAME2-EXTENDED = {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME2 = M17-Y5:N35-C1 |NAME2-IN-CARTOUCHE = yes |NAME3-EXTENDED = {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Times|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|lt=Greco-Roman Times|lt_ja=ギリシャ・ローマ時代|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|lt_de=griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Times|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |NAME3 = M17-Y5:N35-N5:Z1-C1 |IMAGE1 = Min mirror.svg |IMAGE1-WIDTH = 100px |IMAGE1-CAPTION = Amun-Re as Min }} '''Amun-Ra''' unites, as the ancient Egyptian '''king of the gods''', the attributes of {{ill|Ra|en|Ra|qid=Q1252904|lt=Ra|1=Ra}}, {{ill|Min (god)|en|Min (god)|qid=Q192322|lt=Min|1=Min (god)}}, and {{ill|Amun|en|Amun|qid=Q58373|lt=Amun|1=Amun}}. Thus he is the sun, wind, and fertility god in the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian religion|en|Ancient Egyptian religion|qid=Q447131|lt=Ancient Egyptian Religion|1=Ancient Egyptian religion}}. == Background == Amun-Ra is first attested during the {{ill|Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q719634|lt=11th Dynasty|1=Eleventh Dynasty of Egypt}} under {{ill|Mentuhotep II|en|Mentuhotep II|qid=Q296439|lt=Mentuhotep II|1=Mentuhotep II}} in his {{ill|Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|ja|メンツホテップ2世の葬祭寺院|lt=Mortuary Temple|lt_ja=葬祭寺院|de|Totentempel des Mentuhotep II.|lt_de=Totentempel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mortuary Temple of Mentuhotep II|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. A chapel was built in his honor for his new cult. In order to counter the strong veneration of {{ill|Old Kingdom of Egypt|en|Old Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q177819|lt=Old Kingdom|1=Old Kingdom of Egypt}}, the priests of Amun and Ra merged to form Amun-Ra. Amun was often venerated as the “Ra worshiped at Karnak.” His {{ill|synonym|ja|同義語|de|Synonymie|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/synonym|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 類義語|1=Synonym|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q42106}} “breath of life for all things” reaches back in its roots to the Old Kingdom. The “world chamber” and the “{{ill|Obelisk|en|Obelisk|qid=Q170980|lt=obelisk|1=Obelisk}} room” of the {{ill|Sun Temple of Niuserre|ja|ニウセルレの太陽神殿|lt=Sun Temple|lt_ja=太陽神殿|de|Sonnenheiligtum des Niuserre|lt_de=Sonnenheiligtum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sun Temple of Niuserre|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} formed the center of the then Ra cult in connection with the deities Ra and Min. At the same time, the creation motif—transferred to the ruling king—was central, his role at that time as “executor of creation” uniting the characters of Ra and Min. In the {{ill|New Kingdom of Egypt|en|New Kingdom of Egypt|qid=Q180568|lt=New Kingdom|1=New Kingdom of Egypt}}, he became the principal god as “King of the Gods and Lord of the Thrones of both lands.” Under {{ill|Amenhotep IV|ja|アメンホテプ4世|de|Amenophis IV.|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenhotep IV|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アクエンアテン|1=Akhenaten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q81794}}, the veneration of Amun-Ra declined; whether due to a brief period of {{ill|Monolatry|en|Monolatry|qid=Q956859|lt=monolatry|1=Monolatry}} ({{ill|Aten|en|Aten|qid=Q130227|lt=Aten|1=Aten}}) or the spatial separation of {{ill|Pharaoh|en|Pharaoh|qid=Q37110|lt=Pharaoh|1=Pharaoh}} and the {{ill|Elite|en|Elite|qid=Q192797|lt=elite|1=Elite}}, this is not conclusively resolved in scholarly circles. === Representation === [[File:Amun-Ra.jpg|alt=Representation of Amun-Re|thumb]] [[File:Ashmolean_Museum_2022-05-06v.jpg|alt=King Taharqa [ja; de] sacrifices to the ram‐headed “Amun-Re of Gem-Aton”, the local manifestation of the god in Kawa [ja; de], Sudan|thumb]] Since the Middle Kingdom, Amun-Ra of Thebes appeared in three special forms: {{ill|Karnak (disambiguation)|en|Karnak (disambiguation)|qid=Q403182|lt=Amun-Re, Lord of Karnak|1=Karnak (disambiguation)}}, {{ill|Luxor|en|Luxor|qid=Q130514|lt=Amun-Re, Lord of Luxor|1=Luxor}}, and {{ill|Amun-Re-Kamutef|ja|アメン・ラー・カムテフ|de|Amun-Re-Kamutef|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re-Kamutef|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The {{ill|Iconography|en|Iconography|qid=Q208145|lt=iconographic representation|1=Iconography}} initially symbolized the {{ill|attributes|ja|属性（哲学）|lt=attributes|lt_ja=属性|de|Attribut (Philosophie)|lt_de=Attribute|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/attributes|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of Min and Amun. Amun-Ra usually wears the double-feather crown held by a headband. Behind his back, an extended arm wielding a {{ill|Flagellation|en|Flagellation|qid=Q779753|lt=scourge|1=Flagellation}} is visible, symbolizing the subjugation of Egypt’s enemies. The further equation with {{ill|Min-Amun|ja|ミン・アムン|de|Min-Amun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min-Amun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} is first attested during the course of the {{ill|Second Intermediate Period of Egypt|en|Second Intermediate Period of Egypt|qid=Q206715|lt=Second Intermediate Period|1=Second Intermediate Period of Egypt}}, which in the New Kingdom was complemented by {{ill|Kamutef|ja|カムテフ|de|Kamutef|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamutef|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} to form {{ill|Amun-Re-Min-Kamutef|ja|アメン・ラー・ミン・カムテフ|de|Amun-Re-Min-Kamutef|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re-Min-Kamutef|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Accordingly, his {{ill|Iconography|en|Iconography|qid=Q208145|lt=iconographic|1=Iconography}} representation was expanded with the temple of Min and the {{ill|Lattich Garden plants|ja|ラッティッヒ庭園の植物|de|Lattichgarten|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lattich Garden plants|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. In the early {{ill|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q146055|lt=18th Dynasty|1=Eighteenth Dynasty of Egypt}}, finally the depiction of {{ill|Min's Tent|ja|ミンのテント|de|Gottesschatten (Ägyptische Mythologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min's Tent|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was added. === Other Functions === At the latest under {{ill|Hatshepsut|en|Hatshepsut|qid=Q129234|lt=Hatshepsut|1=Hatshepsut}}, Amun-Ra assumed, as a {{ill|chronocrat|ja|クロノクラット|de|Chronokrat|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chronocrat|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, rule over the {{ill|Season of the Emergence|en|Season of the Emergence|qid=Q2037143|lt=Peret|1=Season of the Emergence}} in the {{ill|lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|ja|太陰暦（古代エジプト）|lt=lunar calendar|lt_ja=太陰暦|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|lt_de=Mondkalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lunar calendar (Ancient Egypt)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. With this step, another connection was established with {{ill|Nyuserre Ini|en|Nyuserre Ini|qid=Q269200|lt=Niuserre|1=Nyuserre Ini}} in the {{ill|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Fifth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q269237|lt=5th Dynasty|1=Fifth Dynasty of Egypt}}, who celebrated the {{ill|Sed festival|en|Sed festival|qid=Q604644|lt=Sed festival|1=Sed festival}} in his time with the {{ill|Sunrise|en|Sunrise|qid=Q193294|lt=sunrise|1=Sunrise}} of the first {{ill|New moon|en|New moon|qid=Q108566|lt=new moon|1=New moon}} day in the first Peret month. In the {{ill|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|en|Temple of Seti I (Abydos)|qid=Q1536148|lt=Mortuary Temple|1=Temple of Seti I (Abydos)}}, Amun-Ra bears the title “First of the {{ill|Ennead|en|Ennead|qid=Q233458|lt=Ennead of Heliopolis|1=Ennead}} who is in the midst of Seti I’s mortuary temple.” This title refers to the ancient creator deity {{ill|Atum|en|Atum|qid=Q170569|lt=Atum|1=Atum}}, who was superseded by Ra in the {{ill|Sixth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Sixth Dynasty of Egypt|qid=Q269979|lt=6th Dynasty|1=Sixth Dynasty of Egypt}} as the new head of the Ennead in {{ill|Heliopolis (ancient Egypt)|en|Heliopolis (ancient Egypt)|qid=Q191687|lt=Heliopolis|1=Heliopolis (ancient Egypt)}}. === Temple of Amun-Ra in Karnak === The {{ill|Temple of Amun-Re in Karnak|ja|カルナックのアメン・ラー神殿|de|Tempel des Amun-Re in Karnak|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Temple of Amun-Re in Karnak|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was the largest and richest in the kingdom. Ultimately, Ra reigned in the guise of Amun until the end of the Pharaonic period. However, with the destruction of Thebes in 663 BC by the {{ill|Assyrian people|en|Assyrian people|qid=Q377085|lt=Assyrians|1=Assyrian people}}, {{ill|Osiris|en|Osiris|qid=Q46491|lt=Osiris|1=Osiris}} rose to become the principal god, who then assumed the office of chronocrat over the first Peret month {{ill|Tybi|ja|タイビ|de|Tybi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. === Osiris in the Role of Amun-Ra === In {{ill|Egypt in Greco-Roman Times|ja|ギリシャ・ローマ時代のエジプト|lt=Greco-Roman Times|lt_ja=ギリシャ・ローマ時代|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|lt_de=griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in Greco-Roman Times|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, as part of the {{ill|Osiris Mysteries|ja|オシリス神秘儀式|de|Osiris-Mysterien|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osiris Mysteries|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the 29th {{ill|Choiak|ja|チョイアク|de|Choiak|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choiak|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was added as the {{ill|Isis|en|Isis|qid=Q79876|lt=Kikellia Festival|1=Isis}}. For the {{ill|Ptolemaic dynasty|en|Ptolemaic dynasty|qid=Q131976|lt=Ptolemies|1=Ptolemaic dynasty}}, the celebrations within the framework of the Osiris Mysteries constituted the most important festivals. On the evening of the first day of the month {{ill|Tybi|ja|タイビ|de|Tybi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tybi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with the onset of the Peret season, the {{ill|Horus Festival|ja|ホルス祭|de|Horus-Fest|1=Nehebkau Festival|2=de|3=Nehebkau-Fest|lt=Horus Festival|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} commenced, during which the conception of {{ill|Horus|en|Horus|qid=Q84122|lt=Horus|1=Horus}} was celebrated. One day earlier, on the evening of the 30th of {{ill|Choiak|ja|チョイアク|de|Choiak|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Choiak|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the {{ill|Osiris Festival|ja|オシリス祭|de|Osiris-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Osiris Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} inaugurated the cycle of rebirth, which the Egyptians celebrated with processions of {{ill|Saṃsāra|en|Saṃsāra|qid=Q173324|lt=rebirth|1=Saṃsāra}} from the evening of the 29th of Choiak until the first rays of sunrise on the 1st of Tybi in all temples with the ''entrance of the {{ill|bark|ja|舟|lt=sacred bark of Osiris|lt_ja=聖なるオシリスの舟|de|Barke|lt_de=heiligen Barke des Osiris|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bark|1=boat|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16767330}},'' and after sunrise on the 1st of Tybi the Horus Festival continued in the evening. === Mention in the Bible === In the Bible ({{B|Jer|46|25|qq=}}), Amun-Ra is mentioned as Amon of No. The prophet Jeremiah foretells the destruction of Amun by the God of Israel. == See Also == * {{ill|Amun-Re Festival|ja|アメン・ラー祭|de|Amun-Re-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amun-Re Festival|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|en|List of Egyptian deities|qid=Q1076616|lt=List of Egyptian Gods|1=List of Egyptian deities}} == Literature == * Jan Assmann: ''Ra and Amun. The Crisis of the Polytheistic Worldview in Egypt of the 18th–20th Dynasties'' (= ''Orbis biblicus et orientalis.'' Volume 51). Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht, Fribourg / Göttingen 1983, ISBN 3-7278-0278-2. * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|qid=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}} et al.: ''{{ill|Lexicon of Egyptian Gods and God Names|ja|エジプトの神々と神名の辞典|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexicon of Egyptian Gods and God Names|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.'' Volume 1: ''3 – y.'' Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-644-7, pp. 335–336. * Maria M. Luiselli: ''The Amun-Ra Hymn of P. Boulaq 17 (P. Cairo CG 58038).'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2004, ISBN 3-447-04841-7. * {{ill|Herbert Ricke|en|Herbert Ricke|qid=Q95436|lt=Herbert Ricke|1=Herbert Ricke}}: ''The Kamutef Sanctuary of Hatshepsut and Thutmose III in Karnak. Report on an Excavation in Front of the Mut Temple Area.'' Swiss Institute for Egyptian Construction Research and Archaeology, Cairo 1954. * {{ill|Siegfried Schott|ja|ジークフリード・ショット|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates.'' Verlag der Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur, Mainz / Wiesbaden 1950. * {{ill|Susanne Voß|ja|ズザンネ・フォス|de|Susanne Voß|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanne Voß|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: ''Studies on the Sun Temples of the 5th Dynasty. Significance and Function of a Singular Temple Type in the Old Kingdom.'' Hamburg 2004 (also: Dissertation, University of Hamburg 2000) ([http://www.sub.uni-hamburg.de/opus/volltexte/2004/2100/pdf/Dissertation.pdf PDF; 2.5 MB]). == External Links == {{Commonscat|Amun-Ra|Amun-Re|1=Amun-Ra|2=de|3=Amun-Re|4=fr|5=Amon-Rê|qq=}} {{Normdaten|TYP=p|GND=118648969|LCCN=no2014023319|VIAF=67259133|qq=}} {{translated page|de|Amun-Re|version=247302372|comment=Imported full de history}} [[de:Amun-Re]] [[ja:アメン・ラー]] {{Commons category|Amun-Ra}} [[eo:Amon-Reo]] [[fi:Amon-Ra]] [[fr:Amon-Rê]] [[hr:Amon Ra]] [[it:Amon-Ra]] [[lt:Amonas Ra]] [[mg:Amôna-Ra]] [[nl:Amon-Ra]] [[sh:Amon-Ra]] [[tl:Amun-Re]] {{wikidata link|Q481554}}
